findings.description: Instructional infographic image is missing from the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF page consists almost entirely of a large NCDOR infographic titled 'Instructions for Handwritten Forms' with three visual panels ('Guidelines,' 'Printing,' 'Before Sending'). In the HTML rendering, that infographic is absent and only the later form title/content beginning with 'NCDOR Web 7-24' and 'D-400 Schedule PN-1' is shown.

findings.description: Reading order is altered because page 1 HTML skips the PDF's initial instruction page and begins with later form content.
findings.evidence: The PDF page 1 first presents a standalone instruction graphic ('Do Not Include This Page' and handwritten-form instructions). The HTML page 1 instead starts with the form heading 'D-400 Schedule PN-1 2024 Other Additions and Other Deductions,' so a reader encounters different content first.

findings.description: Overall page layout is not preserved on page 1 due to omission of the full-page graphic section.
findings.evidence: The PDF page 1 is a centered full-page graphic layout with three hanging panels beneath a circular title. The HTML page 1 uses a standard document flow with text headings and horizontal rules, eliminating the original page structure entirely.

findings.description: Form header formatting differs on page 2, with reduced visual emphasis and altered alignment.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF has a bold branded header with 'NCDOR', a large 'D-400 Schedule PN-1' title, a boxed 'DOR Use Only' area, and centered instructional text. The HTML preserves the text but presents it in simpler web styling with less prominent hierarchy and no boxed header treatment.

findings.description: Page 3 header formatting and form-field presentation are simplified in HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 3, the PDF shows 'Page 2', tax year '2024', name/SSN lines, and the Part B heading in a structured form header. The HTML keeps the content but presents it as plain text above the table, with the form-line styling and header emphasis reduced.

findings.description: The HTML includes the instruction/infographic page that the source PDF explicitly marks as excluded.
findings.evidence: PDF first page states "Do Not Include This Page" above the infographic. HTML includes an image with alt text "Instructions for Handwritten Forms infographic", preserving that excluded page as content.

findings.description: A key sentence in the instructions paragraph is altered from 'Line 19h' to 'or Line 19h,' changing the condition for when the schedule must be attached.
findings.evidence: PDF: "If you enter an amount on Form D-400 Schedule PN, Part B, Line 17e or Line 19h, you must attach this schedule..." HTML: "If you enter an amount on Form D-400 Schedule PN, Part B, Line 17e or Line 19h, you must attach this schedule..."

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is not faithful to the document structure because a page/header label is promoted to an h2 above the document title h1.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses

NCDOR Web 7-24

followed by

D-400 Schedule PN-1 2024 Other Additions and Other Deductions

. In the PDF, "NCDOR Web 7-24" is a logo/form code area, not a section heading.

findings.description: The HTML contains nested duplicate structural wrappers, including a second <main> inside the outer <main>, causing section framing duplication not present in the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML shows an outer <main> containing the page header and then another <main> wrapping the body content. The PDF is a continuous two-page form without duplicated container structure.
